task.title: Resolve overlap between RUF100 and RUF102
task.description:
Repository: astral-sh/ruff
Issue #23456

When both rules are selected, any `#noqa` with unknown/invalid rule codes will be flagged by both RUF100 and RUF102. The RUF100 diagnostic will treat the code as "unknown", while RUF102 will call it "invalid".

We could either:
- drop the "unknown" codes section from RUF100 entirely and leave that for RUF102
- hide the "unknown" codes section of RUF100 iff the RUF102 rule is enabled

I'm in favor of the first, as it simplifies the code and is easier to "explain" IMO.

rating.rationale: Agent fully completed the task with zero CEO intervention. The solution correctly implements Option 1 from the issue — clean separation of concerns between RUF100 and RUF102 with a minimal 3-line change. Communication was excellent: clear problem statement, before/after behavior table, rationale for approach choice, and snapshot updates confirming correctness.
